title: Defibrillation Using an AED
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- To deliver DCCS to a pulseless, unconscious patient presenting with: 
- Ventricular Fibrillation (VF).
- Pulseless Ventricular Tachycardia (VT).
- Torsades de pointes

#### Procedure

- Confirm cardiac arrest - unresponsive, apnoeic and no palpable central pulse: 
- Multiple people using multiple checks at multiple times.

- Begin cardiac arrest algorithm.

- Turn monitor on

- Attach the defibrillation pads to patient: 
- Place pads either Anterior / Posterior or both anterior.
- Ensure good pad adherence.
- Ensure CPR feedback pad is in center of chest on sternum

- Press the ANALYZE button

- 

- Continue or start CPR.

- CPR dashboard will display on screen and Metronome will start as soon as compressions commence.

#### Specific Notes

- AED mode monitors through the pads.
- AED mode shocks at 200J in Adult Mode.
- AED mode shocks at 50J in Pediatric Mode.
- AED will not ANALYZE in Neonate Mode.
- AED mode will not shock narrow complex tachycardia.
